conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules, and then I'll ask you some questions about your circumstances.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I can help you assess your eligibility. To start, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Do you have any children that you're responsible for, and if so, how many?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. Their name is Alex, and they're 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 18 years old, I need to ask:

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in approved education. They're studying for their A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to understand your living situation with Alex.

Do you live with Alex, or does Alex live with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. And can you confirm that you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

- Alex is 18 years old and studying A-levels at sixth form college, which counts as approved education
- You can continue to receive Child Benefit for children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training
- You live with Alex in the UK, making you responsible for them
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

Since all these conditions are met, you qualify for Child Benefit.

**Please note:** If Alex's circumstances change (for example, if they leave education, start working 24 hours or more per week, or start claiming certain benefits in their own right), you would need to inform HMRC as this could affect your entitlement.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form college). According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16, BUT if your child is 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if they are under 20 and stay in approved education or training. Alex meets this criteria as they are 18 (under 20) and in approved education. You live with Alex in the UK, making you responsible for them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) with their 18-year-old child Alex, who is studying A-levels at sixth form college. Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit. No one else is claiming for Alex, and all eligibility criteria are met.
